discovery.one_line_purpose: Certified Translation Filing Desk is a validation microsite for an AI-native service concept covering regulatory compliance. It describes the intended workflow, evidence model, and review pattern so that prospective users can evaluate fit before any commercial engagement.
primary_users: compliance officers, regulatory affairs, and internal audit teams
jobs_to_be_done: Reduce cycle time without losing defensibility
Give auditors a single source of truth
discovery.value_prop: Certified Translation Filing Desk turns a scattered regulatory compliance workflow into an evidence-linked, reviewer-signed record — reconstructable on demand, defensible under audit.
competitors: Generic workflow tools bolted onto this domain
discovery.differentiation: Evidence-linked outputs with owner-in-the-loop QA; no fabricated claims; audit trail as a first-class artifact.
discovery.positioning_statement: For compliance officers, regulatory affairs, and internal audit teams who need to run certified translation filing desk with audit-grade defensibility, Certified Translation Filing Desk is an evidence-linked workflow platform that — unlike spreadsheets, generic workflow tools, or open-ended AI chat — guarantees every claim is cited and every artifact is reviewer-signed.

assumptions.statement: The target audience will pay for evidence-linked, human-reviewed outputs over unverified AI generation.
assumptions.revisit_trigger: First 5 design-partner sales calls — reject if willingness-to-pay signal is absent.

assumptions.statement: The workflow can be reconstructed from an owner-supplied evidence pack within one week per design partner.
The vertical tolerates AI-assisted drafts when human review is explicit and audit-traceable.
Single-tenant per customer is not required for the first cohort — logical isolation with row-level auth is acceptable.
assumptions.revisit_trigger: Any prospect with a hard single-tenant SOC/ISO clause.

assumptions.statement: HTML-only blueprint can be upgraded to a manifest-backed run without material scope change.
assumptions.revisit_trigger: First customer walkthrough of the intended workflow.

expert_panel.key_concern: Is the ICP narrow enough to earn a first design-partner cohort?
expert_panel.recommendation: Constrain the ICP to a single regulatory compliance sub-vertical for launch; broaden only after 5 signed design partners.
expert_panel.dissent: May under-price total addressable market and slow fundraising narrative.

expert_panel.key_concern: Are we defaulting to microservices/serverless without justification?
expert_panel.recommendation: Single deployable modular monolith with a documented seam for the highest-volume module.
expert_panel.dissent: May feel unsophisticated to enterprise reviewers who expect a service-mesh diagram.

expert_panel.key_concern: Is the evidence spine visible and trustable in every workflow?
expert_panel.recommendation: Every artifact view must render claim → evidence → reviewer signature in one glance.
expert_panel.dissent: Adds vertical density that non-domain reviewers may call cluttered.

expert_panel.key_concern: Is the audit trail append-only and reconstructable?
expert_panel.recommendation: Event-sourced audit log for all state transitions; snapshots for read models.
expert_panel.dissent: Higher write-path complexity than plain CRUD; discipline required on schema evolution.

expert_panel.key_concern: Are AI outputs grounded, cited, and reviewer-gated?
expert_panel.recommendation: Retrieval-first with source citations, structured output validation, and human-in-the-loop for external delivery.
expert_panel.dissent: Slower than open-ended chat; may frustrate prospects expecting 'agentic magic'.

expert_panel.key_concern: Is the STRIDE surface documented and mitigated?
expert_panel.recommendation: Threat model per module, per-tenant isolation with row-level auth, secrets in managed KMS, dependency scanning in CI.
expert_panel.dissent: Overhead in early prototype; some controls can wait until first paying customer.

expert_panel.key_concern: Is the compliance target realistic for year-one team size?
expert_panel.recommendation: SOC 2 Type I in year one with mapped controls for downstream Type II; defer heavier frameworks until required by a real contract.
expert_panel.dissent: May lock out enterprise buyers requiring HITRUST/ISO on day one.

expert_panel.key_concern: Is the ops model boring enough to run without a dedicated SRE?
expert_panel.recommendation: Single production region + managed database + Vercel/Cloud edge; SLO published as availability + p95 latency only.
expert_panel.dissent: Single region concentrates risk during regional outages.

expert_panel.key_concern: Are the leading indicators wired before launch?
expert_panel.recommendation: Instrument activation, artifact-completion, and reviewer-signoff events; ship dashboards before first design partner.
expert_panel.dissent: Analytics scope-creep can steal weeks from core product.

expert_panel.key_concern: Does the workflow meet WCAG 2.2 AA for every screen a reviewer uses?
expert_panel.recommendation: Enforce keyboard-only walkthrough for every workflow; add axe-core to CI on the reviewer surfaces.
expert_panel.dissent: Density-heavy vertical UIs are hard to ship AA-clean quickly.

expert_panel.key_concern: Is the microsite claim honest and gated on evidence?
expert_panel.recommendation: Regulator-fluent topical-authority content only; no fake reviews, no LocalBusiness identity schema until owner-facts close.
expert_panel.dissent: Slower content velocity than a generic SaaS blog.

expert_panel.key_concern: Is there a written kill criterion?
expert_panel.recommendation: Kill/pivot decision at 90 days if no design-partner LOI or if reviewer-signoff cycle exceeds 5 business days.
expert_panel.dissent: Founders often resist naming kill criteria in writing.

stride.scenario: Attacker attempts to impersonate a reviewer to sign off on a fabricated artifact.
stride.mitigation: SSO with MFA; reviewer signatures bound to a cryptographic session claim, not a form field.

stride.scenario: Historical evidence entries edited after the fact to hide a bad claim.
stride.mitigation: Append-only audit log; hash-chained artifact snapshots; diff view on every reviewer surface.

stride.scenario: Reviewer denies signing off on a delivered artifact.
stride.mitigation: Signed attestations with server-side timestamp + reviewer identity; export bundle includes signature manifest.

stride.scenario: Cross-tenant leak of Regulated-filing, PII, Internal-audit through shared indices, logs, or prompts.
stride.mitigation: Tenant-scoped row-level auth; PII scrubbing in logs; retrieval indices partitioned per tenant.

stride.scenario: Runaway AI job or export exhausts shared workers.
stride.mitigation: Per-tenant concurrency + budget caps; circuit breaker on model calls; degrade-gracefully queue.

stride.scenario: Standard user acquires reviewer or admin capability via a workflow shortcut.
stride.mitigation: Roles stored in a separate table; capability checks server-side; no client-only role checks.

security.privacy_posture: Data-minimization by default; per-tenant isolation; DPA + BAA templates on file; DSAR runbook published.

business_understanding.summary: Certified Translation Filing Desk — Certified Translation Filing Desk is a validation microsite for an AI-native service concept covering regulatory compliance. It describes the intended workflow, evidence model, and review pattern so that prospective users can evaluate fit before any commercial engagement.
business_understanding.customer_profile: compliance officers, regulatory affairs, and internal audit teams
business_understanding.customer_pain: Scattered control evidence across email, spreadsheets, and legacy tools; reviewer bottleneck; no defensible audit trail; regulator inquiries take days to reconstruct.
business_understanding.paid_outcome: Reliably delivered, Compliance officer-signed filing packet — reconstructable on demand, defensible under audit.
business_understanding.value_creation: AI compresses evidence gathering + drafting; humans own signoff, retraction, and public claims; the platform binds them with an append-only audit trail and per-tenant retrieval isolation.
business_understanding.why_ai_native: The evidence-linked drafting + confidence-scored escalation loop is only economical with a bounded AI orchestration layer. A pure-manual agency cannot hit the cycle time; a pure-automated SaaS cannot hit the defensibility bar.
operational_risks: Premature public claim while owner actions open
assumptions: The target audience will pay for evidence-linked, human-reviewed outputs over unverified AI generation.
The workflow can be reconstructed from an owner-supplied evidence pack within one week per design partner.
The vertical tolerates AI-assisted drafts when human review is explicit and audit-traceable.
Single-tenant per customer is not required for the first cohort — logical isolation with row-level auth is acceptable.
HTML-only blueprint can be upgraded to a manifest-backed run without material scope change.

critical_path: ObligationAccepted → EvidenceCollected → DraftReady → ArtifactSigned → ArtifactDelivered → DeliveryConfirmed
exception_flows: DraftProduced with low confidence → EscalationRaised → reviewer manual draft → ArtifactSigned
DeliverArtifact blocked by open owner action → DeliveryBlocked → owner resolves → retry
DeliveryFailed at adapter → customer alert within 1h → Success ticket → retry with backoff
escalation_flows: Reviewer escalates ambiguous artifact to Legal
Success lead escalates silent delivery failure to Owner
Owner escalates regulator inquiry to external counsel
retry_flows: External API failure → exponential backoff with jitter, then dead-letter with owner-visible alert
Model timeout → retry once, then EscalationRaised
Delivery adapter failure → retry with backoff, then customer alert
manual_override_flows: Reviewer may override AI classification and re-route
Owner may force-block a delivery via Compliance ledger
Ops may pull a prompt version at any time (feature flag)

architecture.why: Single team + single regulated vertical per blueprint + strong consistency requirements around signoff / delivery / release-decision favor a monolith. Event-driven internals give us the audit-friendly append-only log without the operational cost of microservices.
rejected_alternatives: Microservices — no independent scaling or team boundary justifies distributed cost yet.
Serverless-workflow-only — reviewer signoff and audit invariants are easier to keep correct in a monolith.
No-code / low-code — cannot enforce OutputValidator, prompt versioning, or per-tenant retrieval isolation with fidelity.
architecture.database_strategy: One managed Postgres; schema-per-context; cross-context reads via published projections; RLS on protected tables.
architecture.event_bus: In-process event dispatcher backed by an outbox table (transactional publish); upgrade path to a real broker if throughput demands.
architecture.queue: Background job queue (managed) for AI runs, delivery adapters, and reindex jobs.
architecture.workflow_engine: None initially — orchestrate via domain events + policies; add a workflow engine if orchestrations exceed 5 sequential steps.
architecture.ai_orchestration: AI Orchestration context owns bounded agents; OutputValidator + prompt registry + tenant-scoped retrieval; retries with backoff.
architecture.rag_layer: Per-tenant partitioned retrieval indices in a managed vector DB; ingestion pipeline in Knowledge Base context.
architecture.file_storage: Managed blob storage with per-tenant prefixes + signed URLs; artifacts hashed on write.
architecture.authn_authz: IdP (Google / Okta) for authn; RBAC via dedicated roles table with server-side checks + RLS.
architecture.admin_dashboard: Ops-facing admin for tenants, users, feature flags, prompt versions.
architecture.client_portal: Customer-facing portal: submit intake, see status, receive artifacts.
architecture.operator_dashboard: Internal: intake queue, reviewer queue, escalation queue, delivery health.
architecture.observability: Structured logs with tenant + request ids; RED metrics per workflow; error tracking with source maps; model-call spans with cost + latency; SLO review weekly.
architecture.audit_logging: Append-only audit log for all state transitions; hash-chained snapshots on signed artifacts.
architecture.deployment: Single production region + multi-AZ managed DB; per-PR preview deploys; migrations gated on review.

target.overview: React shell → Lovable Cloud (Postgres + Auth + Storage + Edge Functions). Every blueprint is a module inside the shell; per-vertical differences live in derived DNA, not in separate deploys. Compliance posture: Domain-specific (agency filings, attestation signers, retention schedules).
target.frontend: Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn primitives; per-blueprint themed via Design DNA; job queue for background pipelines; URL-persisted filter state on audit + diagnostics.
target.backend: Deno-based edge functions per capability. Long generations split into per-item endpoints to stay under IDLE_TIMEOUT.
target.data: Managed Postgres with RLS + JSONB for shape drift. Object storage for source files + generated artifacts.
target.api: REST-ish RPC over edge functions with typed payloads; correlationId on every call for retry/diagnostics.
target.authn_authz: Managed OAuth (Google default). Roles in a dedicated user_roles table + has_role() SECURITY DEFINER function referenced by RLS policies.
target.integrations: GitHub (public read for sync + sources), Lovable AI Gateway (all LLM calls), Cloud Storage (artifacts). No third-party CRM/email yet.
target.background_jobs: blueprintJobQueue in-app: per-slug concurrency limit, exponential backoff + jitter, cancel + invalidate, retention of last error diagnostics.
target.object_storage: Cloud Storage buckets scoped per blueprint slug; signed URLs for artifact download.
target.notifications: In-app toasts + audit trail entries. Email/webhook deferred until owners request it.
target.search: Postgres FTS on blueprint titles + evidence claims; client-side filter for audit trail. Dedicated index deferred.
target.analytics: Lightweight event log in Postgres; dashboard-grade analytics deferred until we have a paying tenant.
target.observability: correlationId per request, per-phase timings, retry timeline in diagnostics drawer, per-blueprint pipeline status panel, JSON report export.
target.deployment: Preview + Production environments; edge functions deploy with the app; Postgres migrations shipped via managed migration tool.
target.security: RLS on every public table; roles in user_roles; secrets in managed vault; OWASP ASVS L1 baseline.

modules.name: Sync & Rollback
modules.responsibility: Daily GitHub sync of blueprint definitions with dry-run, partial-apply, and server-backed rollback of last snapshot.
failure_risks: Partial apply leaving mixed state (mitigated by per-blueprint snapshots)
modules.scaling: Paginate audit trail; snapshot retention window is finite.

modules.name: Design & Architecture DNA
modules.responsibility: Deterministic per-blueprint design + architecture briefs used to gate release readiness.
failure_risks: Vertical → profile drift if new verticals are not mapped

secondary: Client localStorage for UI state (filters, drawer state) — never for auth
data_architecture.cache: React Query + module-level memoization; no dedicated cache service.
data_architecture.search: Postgres FTS on titles + evidence; consider pg_trgm on slugs.
data_architecture.object_storage: Per-slug prefixes; lifecycle rules to prune stale sync snapshots.
data_architecture.schema_strategy: Normalized core + JSONB for evolving shapes (evidence details, capability status).
data_architecture.migrations: Forward-only migrations reviewed in PR; every CREATE TABLE ships GRANTs + RLS enable + policies in the same migration.
data_architecture.retention: Audit trail retained ≥ 1y; sync snapshots retained 90d; error diagnostics retained 30d.
data_architecture.audit_logs: audit_trail table + append-only pattern; export CSV from UI.
data_architecture.soft_delete: Soft-delete evidence via status transition; hard-delete only via owner-initiated purge.
data_architecture.privacy: Only owner-supplied facts persist; filings, attestations, regulator correspondence handled per vertical policy.
data_architecture.encryption: TLS 1.2+ in transit; AES-256 at rest via managed storage.
data_architecture.multi_tenancy: Row-level tenancy keyed on auth.uid() + blueprint slug; RLS policies enforce isolation.

api.style: REST-ish RPC over edge functions with JSON payloads; typed client wrappers.
api.public_vs_internal: Public microsite reads via Postgres RLS-protected queries; internal capability calls via authenticated edge functions.
api.versioning: Version via function name suffix (v1, v2) when breaking; additive changes preferred.
api.rate_limiting: Per-user + per-slug in edge functions; UI-level concurrency caps for AI calls.
api.idempotency: Sync + rollback carry an idempotency key; retries safe.
api.pagination: Cursor pagination on audit trail; offset paging tolerated on small lists.
api.error_format: { code, message, correlationId, retryable, details? } — normalized in client.
api.webhook_security: HMAC-signed webhooks (deferred until we accept inbound webhooks).
api.retries: Exponential backoff + jitter, capped attempts, respect idempotency keys.
api.contract_testing: Zod schemas shared between client + edge; smoke test runner exercises each endpoint.
api.backward_compat: Additive fields only; deprecations announced in audit trail before removal.

security.authn: Managed OAuth (Google default). Session in httpOnly cookie / managed client storage.
security.authz: user_roles table + has_role() SECURITY DEFINER, referenced from RLS policies. Never store roles on profiles.
security.tenant_isolation: RLS on every public table; every query filters by auth.uid() or by an explicit owner grant.
security.secrets: Managed vault; never in client bundle; edge functions read via runtime env.
security.encryption: TLS in transit; AES-256 at rest; column-level encryption only when regulation requires.
security.session: Short-lived access tokens + refresh rotation; SSR cookie parity for edge routes.
security.input_validation: Zod schemas at the edge boundary; reject on unknown fields.
security.api_protection: Rate limits + WAF rules on public endpoints; correlationId logging for abuse forensics.
security.audit_log: Every release decision, evidence toggle, sync, and rollback records actor + timestamp + before/after.
security.admin_access: Admin actions gated behind role check + two-key confirmation on destructive operations.
security.supply_chain: Lockfile pinning + weekly dependency scan; SBOM produced on release.
threat_model: Prompt injection via ingested source files → sanitize + refuse instructions from ingested content.
Cross-tenant read via missing RLS on new table → migration checklist blocks merge.
Rollback abuse to overwrite recent legitimate edits → rollback preview + confirm-typed pattern.
AI cost DOS by repeated regeneration → per-slug rate limits + concurrency cap.
abuse_cases: Malicious owner uploads privileged content into a public microsite field.
Sync run tampered with to inject a slug that overlaps a real blueprint.
Attacker triggers regeneration loop to drive AI cost.
security.zero_trust: Every service call authenticates; no implicit trust between edge functions.

failure_modes: Edge function IDLE_TIMEOUT on long AI generations.
GitHub API rate limit during sync.
Postgres connection saturation during sync fan-out.
reliability.graceful_degradation: Runtime tab modules degrade independently; microsite serves cached last-known-good content when generation fails.
reliability.retry_policy: Exponential backoff + jitter, max 5 attempts, respect Retry-After.
reliability.timeouts: Edge function ≤ 120s wall clock (safety margin under 150s limit); client fetch ≤ 60s per call.
reliability.circuit_breaker: Client-side per-endpoint breaker: after 3 consecutive IDLE_TIMEOUTs, pause 5m and surface to UI.
reliability.queueing: In-app blueprintJobQueue with concurrency limits per slug.
reliability.idempotency: Sync + rollback idempotent via key; generation endpoints idempotent per (slug, doc_key).
reliability.dlq: Failed jobs recorded in error diagnostics; user re-triggers manually (no auto-DLQ needed at current volume).
reliability.transactions: Multi-row writes wrapped in single transaction; audit entry written in the same transaction as the mutation.
reliability.incident_response: correlationId in every log line; on-call runbook per capability module; smoke test replays post-incident.

slos.name: Sync success rate
slos.target: ≥ 99% per daily run over rolling 7 days

slos.name: Generation success rate
slos.target: ≥ 95% per doc across last 7d (excludes provider outages)

mvp_can_stay_simple: No dedicated search or vector index.
In-app job queue; no message broker.
modular_now: Content Pipeline is already isolated behind blueprintJobQueue — future extraction is a 1-day job.
Runtime capabilities are one function per module — swap in isolation.
deferrable: Feature flag service (env-based toggle covers MVP).
scaling.breaks_first: Edge function IDLE_TIMEOUT under multi-doc generation — already addressed by per-doc endpoints; watch for regression.
scaling.db_path: Vertical scale → read replica → partition by tenant if a single tenant dominates load.
scaling.jobs_path: In-app queue → dedicated worker → workflow engine, gated by concurrency + resumability need.
scaling.cache_path: React Query only → HTTP cache headers → CDN edge cache for microsite content.
scaling.search_path: Postgres FTS → pg_trgm → dedicated search only when p95 breaches SLO.
scaling.files_path: Managed object storage → CDN → per-region cache if traffic warrants.
scaling.api_path: Vertical edge function scale → per-capability autoscaling → extract hot module to its own service.
scaling.multi_region: Not planned; introduce only on customer contract with residency requirement.
scaling.cost_control: Per-blueprint AI budget, concurrency cap, smoke-test cache; monthly cost review with per-blueprint attribution.

devops.cicd: Lovable build pipeline; deploys on merge; edge functions ship atomically with the app.
devops.secrets: Managed vault; separate values per environment.
devops.preview_envs: Automatic per branch; seeded with anonymized fixtures.
devops.migrations: Forward-only; migrations reviewed for GRANT + RLS + policies; every table gated by the migration checklist.
devops.rollback: App: redeploy previous build. Data: server-backed rollback per blueprint via rollback-blueprint-sync.
devops.release_style: Continuous deploy with feature flags; canary only when a change touches shared shell.
devops.feature_flags: Env-based booleans at MVP; consider a flag service when we have > 20 flags in flight.
devops.monitoring: Cloud platform metrics + per-function logs + client error reporting.
devops.alerting: SLO burn-rate alerts + IDLE_TIMEOUT rate alert + AI cost anomaly alert.
devops.logs: Structured JSON with correlationId; retained per platform defaults.
devops.error_tracking: Client-side error capture wired to console + in-app diagnostics drawer.
devops.uptime: Synthetic checks on microsite + shell login every 5 minutes.
devops.cost_monitoring: Per-blueprint cost view; alert on 3x baseline over 24h.

testing.unit: Vitest for pure derivations (DNA, business helpers).
testing.integration: Edge function contract tests with recorded fixtures.
testing.contract: Zod schemas shared client + edge; smoke test runner as continuous contract check.
testing.e2e: Playwright against localhost preview for critical flows (sign in, evidence toggle, sync apply).
testing.security: Weekly dependency scan; RLS policy audit script; abuse-case checklist per release.
testing.a11y: Axe checks + keyboard-only smoke on shell components; WCAG 2.2 AA target.
testing.load: k6 scenarios against edge functions before enabling a new capability network-wide.
testing.chaos: Manual fault injection on AI provider (simulate 500s) during release rehearsal.
testing.migration: Every migration runs in preview + dry-run on prod snapshot before apply.
testing.test_data: Deterministic fixtures per vertical; no real PHI/PII ever in fixtures.

observability.logs: Structured JSON with correlationId, phase, attempt, doc_key, slug.
observability.metrics: Per-endpoint latency, error rate, retry count, AI token spend.
observability.audit_events: Release decision, evidence toggle, sync, rollback, cache invalidation.
observability.business_events: Blueprint promoted to ready, first microsite view, first customer-visible export.
observability.error_tracking: In-app diagnostics drawer + persistent per-slug error history.
observability.security_monitoring: Failed auth + rate-limit breach + admin action logs.
alert_thresholds: Sync run failure > 1 in rolling 7 days.
p95 breach on Blueprint detail > 800ms for 15 min.
observability.triage: correlationId → diagnostics drawer → retry timeline → JSON report export → runbook link.

drivers.name: AI generation
drivers.note: Dominant driver; capped by concurrency + per-slug budget.

drivers.name: Edge function invocations
drivers.note: Bursty at sync + generation; idle-to-zero otherwise.

drivers.name: Managed Postgres
drivers.note: Small; scales with audit trail retention.

drivers.name: Object storage
drivers.note: Snapshots + artifacts; lifecycle rules prevent growth.

likely_traps: Regeneration loops on failure (mitigated by circuit breaker).
Audit trail unbounded growth (mitigated by retention policy).
Storing large HTML snapshots per sync (mitigated by delta snapshots).
controls: Per-blueprint AI budget with hard cap.

multi_tenancy.model: Row-level tenancy: one shared Postgres, tenant scope by auth.uid() + blueprint slug.
multi_tenancy.isolation: RLS policies on every public table; policies reference has_role() where role checks are needed.
multi_tenancy.tenant_aware_authz: Every query filters by auth.uid(); admin overrides go through explicit role check + audit entry.
multi_tenancy.tenant_config: Per-slug config stored as JSONB on the blueprint row; no per-tenant deploy.
multi_tenancy.branding: Per-blueprint Design DNA drives theme; no runtime branding upload at MVP.
multi_tenancy.tenant_export: Owner can export evidence + audit trail as JSON/CSV from the UI.
multi_tenancy.tenant_deletion: Owner-initiated purge cascades across blueprint rows + storage prefix; soft-delete window of 30 days.
multi_tenancy.tenant_audit: Per-slug audit trail table view with actor + timestamp on every mutation.
multi_tenancy.noisy_neighbor: Per-slug concurrency cap in blueprintJobQueue; per-slug AI budget.
multi_tenancy.tenant_rate_limits: Edge functions apply per-slug + per-user rate limits.
multi_tenancy.billing: Not billed at MVP; per-blueprint cost attribution feeds the future billing model.
multi_tenancy.why_this_fits: Team size and blueprint scale don't justify schema/db-per-tenant; RLS covers the isolation requirement with negligible ops burden.

privacy_compliance.data_classification: Owner-supplied facts and evidence citations are the sensitive classes; filings, attestations, regulator correspondence per vertical.
privacy_compliance.minimization: Only owner-supplied facts persist; no third-party enrichment; no PII scraping.
privacy_compliance.consent: Consent captured at intake for owner-supplied contact info; microsite visitors get standard cookie/consent banner where required.
privacy_compliance.access_logs: Every admin + edge function invocation logged with correlationId + actor.
privacy_compliance.audit_trails: Immutable append-only audit_trail table; export from UI.
privacy_compliance.retention: Audit ≥ 1y; sync snapshots 90d; error diagnostics 30d; artifacts per lifecycle rule.
privacy_compliance.legal_hold: Deferred until a matter requires it.
privacy_compliance.right_to_delete: Owner-initiated purge honored within 30 days; downstream copies pruned by lifecycle rules.
privacy_compliance.right_to_export: JSON + CSV export for evidence, audit trail, and generated artifacts.
privacy_compliance.sensitive_handling: No PHI/PII in prompts; owner-supplied facts only; secrets in managed vault.
privacy_compliance.boundaries: Domain-specific (agency filings, attestation signers, retention schedules)
privacy_compliance.residency: Single region at MVP; residency contract triggers per-tenant residency planning.
privacy_compliance.vendor_risk: Lovable Cloud + AI Gateway are the only critical vendors; both reviewed for security posture.
privacy_compliance.breach_response: correlationId + audit trail enables scope determination; disclosure per compliance policy within statutory window.
privacy_compliance.admin_controls: Admin actions gated by role check + two-key confirm for destructive operations; every admin session logged.
privacy_compliance.evidence_collection: Access reviews, change management, restore drills produce artifacts filed into the evidence pipeline.

frontend.rendering: SPA with per-route code-split; microsite routes prerender-friendly.
frontend.routing: react-router-dom v6 with URL-persisted filter/drawer state.
frontend.state: React context + useSyncExternalStore for the job queue; localStorage only for UI state, never for auth.
frontend.forms: Controlled components + Zod validation on submit; RHF only where forms grow.
frontend.error_handling: Error boundary at shell + per-panel skeletons + retry affordances.
frontend.components: shadcn primitives + per-blueprint themed panels; deterministic Design DNA drives look.
frontend.design_system: Tailwind semantic tokens (index.css); no hardcoded color utilities in components.
frontend.auth_ui: Managed OAuth callback via Cloud client; session hydration before protected routes render.
frontend.authz_aware_ui: UI hides actions the current role cannot perform; server-side check is authoritative.
frontend.a11y: WCAG 2.2 AA target; visible focus rings; keyboard shortcuts in diagnostics drawer.
frontend.i18n: Copy budgets assume +35% expansion for DE/FR; Intl APIs for dates/currencies.
frontend.performance: Route-level code split; lazy-load Runtime tab; memoize DNA derivations.
frontend.bundling: Vite defaults; per-route lazy imports for heavy panels.
frontend.testing: Vitest for unit; Playwright for critical flows.
frontend.offline: Not required; last-known-good served from React Query cache.
frontend.realtime: Not required at MVP; audit trail is polled on interaction.

backend.framework: Deno-based edge functions on Lovable Cloud, one function per capability.
backend.domain: Blueprint, Evidence, OwnerAction, SyncRun, RuntimeCapability, ChatMessage.
backend.services: Pure functions kept out of edge boundary; shared logic imported from a common module.
backend.repositories: Thin Postgres wrappers; RLS enforces tenant scope.
backend.validation: Zod at the edge boundary; reject unknown fields.
backend.authorization: has_role() SECURITY DEFINER in Postgres; edge function also asserts role for defense in depth.
backend.jobs: In-app blueprintJobQueue on the client for user-triggered pipelines; server-side cron only for daily sync.
backend.events: Domain events emitted to audit_trail; no external broker.
backend.files: Signed URLs from Cloud Storage; virus scan on upload (deferred until user uploads exist).
backend.email_sms: Deferred; owner-configured inbox required before enabling outbound mail.
backend.scheduled: Daily GitHub sync via scheduled function; retention prune weekly.
backend.errors: Normalized error envelope { code, message, correlationId, retryable, details? }.
backend.logging: Structured JSON logs with correlationId, phase, attempt, slug.
backend.di: Not required at current size; explicit imports.
backend.testing: Contract tests per function with recorded fixtures + smoke-test runner.

adrs.decision: Adopt modular monolith as the network-wide architecture style
adrs.context: Small team maintaining many blueprints with shared shell, evidence discipline, and per-vertical trust variation.
adrs.why: Preserves a single audit boundary and deploy cadence while allowing edge functions for burst workloads.
consequences: Row-level tenant isolation carries the security load
risks: A rogue blueprint can regress network shell performance
adrs.reversal: Extract a module to its own deploy only when its SLO diverges from the network shell.
adrs.revisit_when: A blueprint acquires a divergent SLO, a second team joins, or the shell deploy time exceeds 10 minutes.

adrs.decision: Managed Postgres as the sole primary datastore
adrs.context: All entities are relational (blueprints, evidence, sync runs, articles, audit).
adrs.chosen: Postgres (Cloud managed) with JSONB for semi-structured fields
adrs.why: Relational integrity + row-level security satisfies audit, tenancy, and reporting; JSONB absorbs shape drift.
consequences: RLS policies are the primary tenancy control
Full-text search via Postgres FTS until it stops scaling
adrs.reversal: Introduce a read-replica or a dedicated search index only when p95 breaches SLO.
adrs.revisit_when: FTS p95 > SLO for 2 consecutive weeks, or a genuine RAG surface appears.

adrs.decision: Per-document edge functions for long-running AI generation
adrs.context: IDLE_TIMEOUT (150s) on monolithic legal-docs generator forced this split.
adrs.chosen: Per-document endpoints with client-side fan-out + retries
adrs.why: Keeps each invocation under the timeout, isolates failures, enables partial success reporting.
risks: Client back-pressure if fan-out is too wide
adrs.reversal: Move to a real workflow engine when we cross ~10 concurrent long jobs per blueprint.
adrs.revisit_when: Concurrent long-running jobs > 10 per blueprint, or client-side orchestration becomes buggy.

adrs.context: Vertical compliance posture: Domain-specific (agency filings, attestation signers, retention schedules).
adrs.chosen: Evidence-gated ready — release requires resolved owner actions + verified evidence
adrs.why: Regulated verticals cannot ship on self-declaration; evidence provides defensibility.
adrs.reversal: Introduce a 'ready-with-caveats' state only if the network stalls on this gate.
adrs.revisit_when: > 30% of blueprints stuck in owner-action state for > 30 days.

adrs.decision: Defer AI adoption until a specific evidence-generation need arises
adrs.context: AI is powerful but adds cost, latency, and auditability burden.
adrs.why: Vertical does not currently justify AI-shaped complexity.
adrs.reversal: Introduce AI only for a scoped generation task.
adrs.revisit_when: A specific generation task appears with clear source grounding.

adrs.decision: Managed OAuth (Google) with roles in a dedicated user_roles table
adrs.context: Storing roles on the profile row invites privilege-escalation bugs; RLS policies must reference a stable role source.
adrs.chosen: user_roles table + has_role() SECURITY DEFINER, referenced by RLS
adrs.why: Prevents recursive RLS, isolates authz decisions, satisfies audit review.
risks: Role drift if grants are not audited
adrs.reversal: Swap SECURITY DEFINER function for an IdP claim without changing policies.
adrs.revisit_when: Enterprise SSO / SAML contract signed, or role count exceeds ~10.

adrs.decision: Single-tenant per blueprint slug with row-level isolation
adrs.context: Blueprints share infra but must never cross-read evidence, sync history, or generated artifacts.
adrs.chosen: Shared DB + RLS keyed on auth.uid() and blueprint slug
adrs.why: Simplest operable model at current scale; migration cost stays near zero.
adrs.reversal: Extract a specific tenant to its own schema when contract requires it.
adrs.revisit_when: First enterprise customer with a residency or dedicated-DB clause.

dangerous_debt: Missing RLS on any public table

build: Automated smoke test per blueprint on cache invalidation
Contract tests for every edge function
triggers_to_change: A single blueprint accounts for > 30% of AI spend
acceptable_debt: Manual runbook execution for rare failures

build: Optional workflow engine adapter behind the current queue interface
Read replica for Postgres if analytics queries interfere
avoid: Splitting Blueprint Core into services without SLO justification
monitor: Fan-out concurrency vs edge function limits
triggers_to_change: New team joins with independent release cadence

build: Extract Content Pipeline to a dedicated service if it dominates deploys
Search index (Postgres FTS → dedicated) once FTS p95 breaches SLO
defer: Multi-region until a customer contract requires it

build: Formal SOC 2 evidence pipeline (access reviews, change management)
Tenant-scoped encryption keys where regulation requires
DR drill quarterly with restore proof
avoid: Custom compliance frameworks; ride managed platform attestations
acceptable_debt: Manual quarterly access review with checklist
dangerous_debt: Ad-hoc admin access without approval trail

flagged.why: Team size + workload shape don't justify it.
Single deploy cadence + shared audit boundary.
Sources are small + structured; deterministic retrieval works.
Audit trail table already provides the needed reconstructibility.
In-app queue covers current concurrency needs.
In-app queue + audit trail cover the fan-out cases.
Managed OAuth + user_roles cover the model.
React Query covers the read-heavy paths.
No analytics contract; Postgres analytics queries suffice.
flagged.simpler: Read replica if the primary is hurt.

risks.risk: Edge function IDLE_TIMEOUT on long generations
risks.mitigation: Per-document endpoints + client-side retries with exponential backoff.
risks.detection: Smoke test runner + diagnostics drawer flags IDLE_TIMEOUT.
risks.escalation: Sustained > 3/day for one blueprint → open incident.
risks.fallback: Fall back to last-known-good cached artifact; pause auto-generation for the affected blueprint.

risks.risk: Duplicate slugs in SEED causing UI regressions
risks.mitigation: mergedSeed dedupes by slug; add lint on SEED at build time.
risks.detection: React duplicate-key warning; per-slug uniqueness assertion in tests.
risks.fallback: Runtime dedupe in mergedSeed keeps first occurrence.

risks.risk: Ungrounded AI output shipped to microsite
risks.mitigation: Citation-first prompts; integrity check gates Seed Articles view.
risks.detection: Integrity report failing count > 0 blocks display.
risks.escalation: Any customer-visible ungrounded claim → rollback the blueprint.
risks.fallback: Auto-hide the article + surface owner action to regenerate with stricter prompt.

risks.risk: Cross-tenant data leak via missing RLS on new table
risks.mitigation: Every CREATE TABLE ships with GRANT + ENABLE RLS + policies in the same migration.
risks.fallback: Revoke Data API grants on affected table; restore from PITR if data was modified.

risks.risk: AI cost runaway on a single blueprint
risks.mitigation: Per-slug rate limits + smoke-test cache + concurrency cap in UI.
risks.detection: Cost monitoring dashboard; per-blueprint spend alert at 3x baseline.
risks.fallback: Disable AI for the offending blueprint via feature flag; serve last-known-good.

risks.risk: AI provider outage or model deprecation
risks.mitigation: Per-capability fallback model + retry with backoff; abstract via Lovable AI Gateway.
risks.detection: Elevated 5xx or empty completions; smoke test failing across blueprints.
risks.escalation: Sustained > 30 min → switch fallback model; notify owners.
risks.fallback: Serve cached artifacts + disable AI-only capabilities until restored.

risks.mitigation: Evidence-first release gate + audit trail export from UI.
risks.detection: Missing audit entries surfaced in periodic reconciliation report.
risks.escalation: Regulator-visible gap → incident + disclosure per policy.
risks.fallback: Freeze affected blueprint's release state; produce backfill evidence pack.

risks.mitigation: Deterministic DNA modules keep decisions in code, not in one head; runbooks per capability.
risks.escalation: > 1 critical path with no backup → hire or contract.
risks.fallback: Freeze non-critical changes; document current state before further work.

risks.risk: Audit trail gaps on release decisions
risks.mitigation: Every mutation writes audit entry in the same transaction.
risks.detection: Audit trail row count vs mutation count reconciliation.
risks.fallback: Reconstruct from Postgres WAL + application logs; disclose per compliance policy.

rules: Keep business logic out of UI components — derivations live in lib/*, panels only render.
Do not introduce a new service without a clear owner and a scaling reason.
All external integrations must have retries, timeouts, and failure handling.
All sensitive actions must be auditable in the same transaction that performs them.
All background jobs must be idempotent.
All APIs must return the normalized error envelope.
All tenant-scoped queries must enforce tenant isolation via RLS — never trust the client.
All expensive AI calls must be logged, capped, and observable.
All schema changes must be reversible or safely migratable — no destructive drops without a rollout plan.
All critical workflows must have observability: correlationId, phase timings, retry timeline.
Every public table ships with GRANT + ENABLE RLS + policies in the same migration.
Every AI span carries a citation; no citation, no ship.
Every destructive action requires typed confirmation.

recommendation.stack: React + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn on the client; Deno edge functions + managed Postgres (RLS) + object storage on the server; Lovable AI Gateway (unused in this vertical).

AI cost runaway on a single blueprint.
